steps.title: Recognize and factor the common expression
steps.content:
The expression $(x^2 - x)$ appears in both terms. Factor it out:

$$2(x^2 - x) + 3(x^2 - x) = (2+3)(x^2 - x)$$

Combine the coefficients: $(2+3)(x^2 - x) = 5(x^2 - x)$

steps.title: Distribute the coefficient
steps.content:
Multiply 5 by each term inside the parentheses:

$$5(x^2 - x) = 5x^2 - 5x$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
